docs.text: Sets the inventory height of the grenade item

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During item definition

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text: ITEM.height = 1  -- Takes 1 slot height

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Sets the health value for the item when it's dropped as an entity in the world

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: During item definition (used when item is spawned as entity) Notes: - Defaults to 100 if not specified - When the item entity takes damage, its health decreases - Item is destroyed when health reaches 0 - Only applies if ITEM.CanBeDestroyed is true (controlled by config)

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage

docs.title: Complete Examples
docs.text: The following examples demonstrate how to use all the properties and methods together to create complete definitions.

docs.title: Complete Item Example
docs.text:
Below is a comprehensive example showing how to define a complete item with all available properties and methods.

            ITEM.name = "Fragmentation Grenade"

            ITEM.desc = "A deadly fragmentation grenade"

            ITEM.category = "itemCatGrenades"

            ITEM.model = "models/weapons/w_eq_fraggrenade.mdl"

            ITEM.class = "weapon_frag"

            ITEM.width = 1  -- Takes 1 slot width

            ITEM.height = 1  -- Takes 1 slot height

            ITEM.health = 250  -- Item can take 250 damage before being destroyed

            ITEM.DropOnDeath = true  -- Drops on death

    -- Basic item identification
        ITEM.name = "Fragmentation Grenade"                    -- Display name shown to players
        ITEM.desc = "A deadly fragmentation grenade"           -- Description text
        ITEM.category = "itemCatGrenades"                      -- Category for inventory sorting
        ITEM.model = "models/weapons/w_eq_fraggrenade.mdl"     -- 3D model for the grenade
        ITEM.class = "weapon_frag"                             -- Weapon class to give when used
        ITEM.width = 1                                         -- Inventory width (1 slot)
        ITEM.height = 1                                        -- Inventory height (1 slot)
        ITEM.health = 100                                      -- Health when dropped (default: 100)
        ITEM.DropOnDeath = true                                -- Drops on death

docs.title: Complete Item Example
docs.text:
Below is a comprehensive example showing how to define a complete item with all available properties and methods.

            ITEM.name = "Police Uniform"

    end

    function ITEM:removePart(client)
        local char = client:getChar()
        self:setData("equip", false)
        if client.removePart then client:removePart(self.uniqueID) end
        -- Remove attribute boosts
    end

    function ITEM:onCanBeTransfered(_, newInventory)

    end

        if self:getData("equip") and self.player.addPart then self.player:addPart(self.uniqueID) end

    function ITEM:onRemoved()

        local receiver = inv.getReceiver and inv:getReceiver()
        if IsValid(receiver) and receiver:IsPlayer() and self:getData("equip") then self:removePart(receiver) end
    end

            ITEM:hook("drop", function(item)

            end)

function ITEM.postHooks:drop()
        local client = self.player
        if not client or not IsValid(client) then return end
        if client:HasWeapon(self.class) then
            client:notifyErrorLocalized("invalidWeapon")
            client:StripWeapon(self.class)
        end
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Handles weapon loading on player spawn

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When player spawns with equipped weapon

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
function ITEM:onLoadout()
        if self:getData("equip") then
            local client = self.player
            if not client or not IsValid(client) then return end
            local weapon = client:Give(self.class, true)
            if IsValid(weapon) then
                client:RemoveAmmo(weapon:Clip1(), weapon:GetPrimaryAmmoType())
                weapon:SetClip1(self:getData("ammo", 0))
            else
                lia.error(L("weaponDoesNotExist", self.class))
            end
        end
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Saves weapon ammo data

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When saving the weapon item

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
function ITEM:OnSave()
        local client = self.player
        if not client or not IsValid(client) then return end
        local weapon = client:GetWeapon(self.class)
        if IsValid(weapon) then self:setData("ammo", weapon:Clip1()) end
    end

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: 💡 Example Usage
docs.text:
function ITEM:getName()
        local weapon = weapons.GetStored(self.class)
        if weapon and weapon.PrintName then return language.GetPhrase(weapon.PrintName) end
        return self.name
    end

docs.title: Complete Examples
docs.text: The following examples demonstrate how to use all the properties and methods together to create complete definitions.

docs.title: Complete Item Example
docs.text:
Below is a comprehensive example showing how to define a complete item with all available properties and methods.

        ITEM.name = "Pistol"

        ITEM.desc = "A standard issue pistol"

        ITEM.category = "weapons"

        ITEM.model = "models/weapons/w_pistol.mdl"

        ITEM.class = "weapon_pistol"

        ITEM.width = 2  -- Takes 2 slot width

        ITEM.height = 2  -- Takes 2 slot height

        ITEM.health = 250  -- Weapon can take 250 damage before being destroyed

        ITEM.isWeapon = true

        ITEM.RequiredSkillLevels = {}  -- No skill requirements

        ITEM.DropOnDeath = true  -- Drops on death

        function ITEM.postHooks:drop()
            local client = self.player
            if not client or not IsValid(client) then return end
            if client:HasWeapon(self.class) then
                client:notifyErrorLocalized("invalidWeapon")
                client:StripWeapon(self.class)
            end
        end

        ITEM:hook("drop", function(item)
            local client = item.player
            if not client or not IsValid(client) then return false end
            if IsValid(client:GetRagdollEntity()) then
                client:notifyErrorLocalized("noRagdollAction")
                return false
            end
            -- Handle equipped weapon removal
        end)

        function ITEM:OnCanBeTransfered(_, newInventory)
            if newInventory and self:getData("equip") then return false end
            return true
        end

        function ITEM:onLoadout()
            if self:getData("equip") then
                local client = self.player
                if not client or not IsValid(client) then return end
                local weapon = client:Give(self.class, true)
                if IsValid(weapon) then
                    client:RemoveAmmo(weapon:Clip1(), weapon:GetPrimaryAmmoType())
                    weapon:SetClip1(self:getData("ammo", 0))
                else
                    lia.error(L("weaponDoesNotExist", self.class))
                end
            end
        end

        function ITEM:OnSave()
            local client = self.player
            if not client or not IsValid(client) then return end
            local weapon = client:GetWeapon(self.class)
            if IsValid(weapon) then self:setData("ammo", weapon:Clip1()) end
        end

        function ITEM:getName()
            local weapon = weapons.GetStored(self.class)
            if weapon and weapon.PrintName then return language.GetPhrase(weapon.PrintName) end
            return self.name
        end

    -- Basic item identification
    ITEM.name        = "Pistol"                       -- Display name shown to players
    ITEM.desc        = "A standard issue pistol"      -- Description text
    ITEM.category    = "weapons"                      -- Category for inventory sorting
    ITEM.model       = "models/weapons/w_pistol.mdl"  -- 3D model for the weapon
    ITEM.class       = "weapon_pistol"                -- Weapon class to give when equipped
    ITEM.width       = 2                              -- Inventory width (2 slots)
    ITEM.height      = 2                              -- Inventory height (2 slots)
    ITEM.DropOnDeath = true                           -- Drops on death

docs.title: About
docs.text:
About

Name: Advertisements

Description:

Implements a paid /advert command for server-wide announcements. Messages are colored, logged, and throttled by a cooldown to curb spam.

Main Features
  • Adds a paid /advert command (alias: /advertisement) for server-wide announcements
  • Adds colored broadcast messages across the server
  • Adds message logging for moderation purposes
  • Adds configurable cooldown system to prevent spam
  • Adds configurable pricing system
  • Adds notifications when players lack funds or are on cooldown

DOWNLOAD HERE

docs.title: Version 1.5
docs.text:
• Enhanced documentation with detailed configuration options including AdvertPrice and AdvertCooldown settings with usage guidelines
  • Added comprehensive hooks documentation for the AdvertSent hook with multiple complexity examples
  • Improved documentation structure and formatting for better readability

docs.title: Version 1.4
docs.text: • Added comprehensive configuration documentation

docs.title: Version 1.3
docs.text: • Added comprehensive hooks documentation

docs.title: Version 1.1
docs.text: • Created docs

docs.title: Version 1.0
docs.text: • Initial Release

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the Advertisements module.

Overview

The Advertisements module lets you set how much it costs to send server-wide messages and how long players must wait between messages. This helps prevent spam while allowing important announcements.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How much money players pay to send a server-wide advertisement message.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Int

docs.title: 📊 Range
docs.text: Minimum: 1 Maximum: 1000

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Lower values (1-5) make ads cheap but may cause spam
  • Medium values (10-50) work well for most servers
  • Higher values (100+) make ads expensive and rare

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How many seconds players must wait between sending advertisement messages.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Int

docs.title: 📊 Range
docs.text: Minimum: 1 Maximum: 3600

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Short cooldowns (1-10 seconds) allow quick messages but don't prevent spam
  • Medium cooldowns (20-60 seconds) are recommended for most servers
  • Long cooldowns (300+ seconds) make advertisements rare and special

docs.title: Hooks
docs.text:
Hooks provided by the Advertisements module for server-wide announcements.

Overview

The Advertisements module provides a paid server-wide announcement system that allows players to broadcast messages to all connected players. It implements spam prevention through cooldowns, message logging for administrative oversight, and colored message formatting for better visibility. The module integrates with the economy system for paid advertisements and includes comprehensive hook support for customizing advertisement behavior, validation, and processing workflows.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a player successfully sends an advertisement message to the server.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After an advertisement has been sent, logged, and displayed to all players.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player who sent the advertisement message string The advertisement message content

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
hook.Add("AdvertSent", "LogAdvertisements", function(client, message)
    print(client:Name() .. " sent an advertisement: " .. message)
end)

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
hook.Add("AdvertSent", "TrackAdvertisementStats", function(client, message)
    if not client.advertCount then client.advertCount = 0 end
    client.advertCount = client.advertCount + 1

    -- Track advertisement length
    local stats = client:getNetVar("advertStats", {})
    stats.totalLength = (stats.totalLength or 0) + #message
    stats.count = client.advertCount
    client:setNetVar("advertStats", stats)
end)

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
hook.Add("AdvertSent", "AdvancedAdvertisementSystem", function(client, message)
    -- Store advertisement in database
    local char = client:getChar()
    if char then
        lia.db.insert("advertisements", {
            steamid = client:SteamID(),
            charid = char:getID(),
            message = message,
            timestamp = os.time()
        })
    end

    -- Check for special keywords and trigger events
    local keywords = {"sale", "wanted", "hiring", "selling"}
    for _, keyword in ipairs(keywords) do
        if string.find(string.lower(message), keyword) then
            hook.Run("AdvertKeywordTriggered", client, keyword, message)
        end
    end

    -- Send notification to admins
    for _, admin in player.Iterator() do
        if admin:IsAdmin() then
            admin:notify(client:Name() .. " sent: " .. message)
        end
    end
end)

  • Adds multi-language support for all text elements
  • Adds integration with restraint systems

Overview

The AFK Protection module automatically detects when players are away and protects them from being tied up, arrested, or exploited. You can set how long players must be inactive before being marked as AFK, and whether protection is enabled.

docs.text: Int

docs.title: 📊 Range
docs.text: Minimum: 30 Maximum: 600

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Short durations (30-60 seconds) detect AFK quickly but may mark players during slow roleplay
  • Medium durations (180-300 seconds) work well for most servers
  • Long durations (300+ seconds) only mark players after extended inactivity

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Turns AFK protection on or off. When enabled, AFK players cannot be tied up or arrested.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Boolean

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text: true

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Enabled (true) is recommended - protects players who step away
  • Disabled (false) still marks players as AFK but doesn't protect them

Name: Alcoholism

Description:

Adds drinkable alcohol that increases a player's intoxication level. High BAC blurs vision and slows movement until the effect wears off.

Main Features

  • Adds player notification when reaching DrunkNotifyThreshold
  • Adds configurable BAC settings and degradation rates
  • Adds multiple drink items with varying strength (vodka, whiskey, wine, beer, etc.)

docs.title: Hooks
docs.text:
Hooks provided by the Alcoholism module for managing blood alcohol content (BAC) and alcohol consumption effects.

Overview

The Alcoholism module implements a comprehensive intoxication system where players can consume alcoholic beverages that progressively increase their Blood Alcohol Content (BAC). As BAC rises, players experience visual impairments like screen blurring, movement slowdown, and other debilitating effects. The system includes BAC thresholds for different intoxication levels, automatic metabolism over time, and extensive hook integration for customizing alcohol effects, consumption mechanics, and sobriety recovery processes.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a player consumes an alcohol item.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After a player successfully drinks an alcohol item and their BAC has been increased.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player who consumed the alcohol item Item The alcohol item that was consumed

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called whenever a player's blood alcohol content (BAC) value changes.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After the BAC value has been updated on the server, whether it increased or decreased.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player whose BAC changed newBac number The new BAC value (0-100)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a player's BAC value increases.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After BAC has been increased but before BACChanged is called.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player whose BAC increased oldBac number The previous BAC value newBac number The new BAC value

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.text: Server

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player whose BAC was reset

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a player's BAC reaches a specific threshold level.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When BAC crosses a defined threshold value (e.g., 30%, 50%, 80%).

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player who reached the threshold bac number The current BAC value threshold number The threshold that was reached

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.text: Server

docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player whose BAC decreased newBac number The new BAC value after decrease

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After BAC reset has been completed.

docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player whose BAC was reset

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called before a player's BAC is decreased during the degradation cycle.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: Before BAC degradation is processed, allowing modification of the decrease rate.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player whose BAC will decrease bac number The current BAC value before decrease

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.text: Server

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player The player whose BAC will be reset

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil or boolean - Return false to prevent reset

Overview

The Auto Restarter module provides automated server restart functionality with scheduled maintenance cycles and player notification systems. It displays countdown timers to give players advance warning of impending restarts, allowing them to save progress and prepare for the map change. The module includes configurable restart intervals, emergency restart capabilities, and comprehensive hook support for integrating with other systems that need to respond to server restart events.

Name: Captions

Description:

Offers an API for timed on-screen captions suited for tutorials or story events. Captions can be triggered from the server or client and last for a chosen duration.

Main Features
  • Adds an API for timed on-screen captions (lia.caption.start())
  • Adds support for both client and server use
  • Adds an easy way to deliver story prompts or tutorial messages
  • Adds admin commands to send captions to players
  • Adds duration control for each caption (default: 5 seconds)
  • Adds network synchronization for caption display

Overview

The Captions module provides a comprehensive API for displaying timed on-screen text overlays suitable for tutorials, story events, and narrative sequences. It supports both server and client-triggered captions with customizable duration, positioning, and styling. The module includes built-in commands for caption management and extensive hook integration for synchronizing caption events across players and customizing caption behavior.

docs.title: Caption Library
docs.text:
Helpers to show and hide Closed Captions for players or locally on the client.

Overview

The caption library provides a simple API for displaying timed on-screen captions/subtitles. It supports both server-side and client-side usage, allowing captions to be triggered from either realm. Captions are displayed for a specified duration and can be manually stopped before the duration expires. The library is ideal for tutorials, story events, or any scenario where temporary on-screen text is needed.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Begin displaying a caption for a duration.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When temporarily showing on-screen captions/subtitles for tutorials, story events, or notifications.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player Recipient (Server variant only) text string Caption text to display duration number Seconds to show the caption

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • void

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server and Client variants exist.

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Show a caption on the client
    lia.caption.start("Welcome", 3)

    -- Simple: Show a caption to a player (Server)
    lia.caption.start(ply, "Sector sweep in progress", 5)

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Show caption with dynamic text (Client)
    local message = "Loading " .. data.name .. "..."
    lia.caption.start(message, 2.5)

    -- Medium: Show caption to multiple players (Server)
    for _, ply in ipairs(player.GetAll()) do
        if ply:IsValid() then
            lia.caption.start(ply, "Event starting soon", 5)
        end
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Conditional caption display with validation
    local function showCaption(player, text, duration, condition)
        if not IsValid(player) then return end
        if condition and player:IsValid() then
            local finalText = text
            if player:GetNWBool("isAdmin", false) then
                finalText = "[ADMIN] " .. text
            end
            lia.caption.start(player, finalText, duration or 3)
            hook.Run("OnCaptionShown", player, finalText, duration)
        end
    end
    showCaption(ply, "Mission complete", 5, ply:Alive())

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Stop an active caption before its duration expires.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When you need to immediately hide a currently displaying caption.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description client Player Recipient (Server variant only, optional on client)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • void

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server and Client variants exist.

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Stop caption on client
    lia.caption.finish()

    -- Simple: Stop caption for a player (Server)
    lia.caption.finish(ply)

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Stop caption after condition
    if condition then
        lia.caption.finish()
    end

    -- Medium: Stop captions for all players (Server)
    for _, ply in ipairs(player.GetAll()) do
        lia.caption.finish(ply)
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Stop captions with validation and hooks
    local function stopCaptionSafely(player)
        if not IsValid(player) then return end
        if SERVER then
            lia.caption.finish(player)
            hook.Run("OnCaptionStopped", player)
        else
            lia.caption.finish()
            hook.Run("OnCaptionStopped")
        end
    end
    stopCaptionSafely(ply)

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text:
{
    ["Rules"] = {
        URL = "https://example.com/rules",
        shouldOpenIngame = true
    },
    ["Discord"] = {
        URL = "https://discord.gg/2QyjggCUx4",
        shouldOpenIngame = false
    },
    ["Content"] = {
        URL = "https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/managecollection/?id=3367324267",
        shouldOpenIngame = false
    },
    ["SteamGroup"] = {
        URL = "https://steamcommunity.com/groups/partumverse",
        shouldOpenIngame = false
    }
}

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A table mapping link names (keys) to URL configuration tables (values). Each URL configuration contains: - URL (string) - The web address to open - shouldOpenIngame (boolean) - Whether to open the URL in the in-game browser (true) or external browser (false)

Overview

The Community Commands module provides an extensible system for community-related chat commands that open URLs, share workshop content, and access documentation. It supports configurable command names with localization, custom URL management, and seamless integration with Steam Workshop and community resources. The module includes comprehensive hook integration for tracking command usage and customizing URL opening behavior.

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

The module includes comprehensive hook integration for customizing cursor behavior, rendering, and interaction events.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the Cutscenes module.

Overview

The Cutscenes module lets you create cinematic scenes with images, sounds, and text. Perfect for server introductions, event announcements, or storytelling.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How many seconds it takes to fade between cutscene scenes.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Number

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Short delays (0.5-1 second) create quick transitions
  • Medium delays (2-3 seconds) work well for most cutscenes
  • Long delays (4+ seconds) create slow, dramatic transitions

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Defines your cutscenes. Each cutscene can have multiple scenes with images, sounds, and subtitles.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text: Example cutscenes including "Mistery Info", "Battle Intro", and "Victory Celebration".

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: Each cutscene contains scenes with: - image (string) - Image URL or file path - sound (string) - Sound file path (optional) - subtitles (table) - Array of subtitle entries with: - text (string) - The subtitle text - font (string) - Font name like "liaSubtitleFont" - duration (number) - How long to show it in seconds - color (Color) - Text color (optional) - songFade (boolean) - Fade out music at end (optional)

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Use web URLs (like Imgur) for images or local file paths
  • Subtitle duration: allow about 1 second per 3-4 words
  • Use "liaSubtitleFont" for regular text or "liaTitleFont" for emphasis
  • Keep cutscenes short so players don't skip them

docs.title: Hooks
docs.text:
Hooks provided by the Cutscenes module for managing cutscene playback and display.

Overview

The Cutscenes module provides a flexible framework for creating and playing scripted cinematic sequences with synchronized camera movement across all clients. It supports table-defined scenes, player skip controls, administrative commands for cutscene management, and comprehensive hook integration for customizing cutscene behavior and extending cinematic functionality.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a cutscene has completely finished playing.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After the fade-out animation completes and all cutscene elements are removed.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id string The identifier of the cutscene that ended

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a specific scene within a cutscene ends.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After a scene's duration expires and before the next scene starts (or cutscene ends).

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id string The identifier of the cutscene scene table The scene data that just ended

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a specific scene within a cutscene starts.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When a new scene begins playing within the cutscene sequence.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id string The identifier of the cutscene scene table The scene data that just started

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a cutscene begins playing.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When the cutscene playback is initiated, before any scenes are displayed.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id string The identifier of the cutscene that started ply Player, optional The player who started the cutscene (server-side only)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Client (id only) or Server (ply, id)

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a subtitle within a cutscene scene starts displaying.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When a subtitle begins showing, including sound playback if configured.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description id string The identifier of the cutscene subtitle table The subtitle data containing text, color, font, and sound

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.text: Client

docs.text:
Configuration options for the Donator module.

Overview

The Donator module lets you give special benefits to donators and user groups. You can set character limits, inventory sizes, group assignments, and weapon access.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Sets how many characters each user group can have.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text:
{
    ["founder"] = 10,
    ["superadmin"] = 3,
    ["admin"] = 3,
    ["user"] = 2
}

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A table mapping user group names to character limit numbers.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Add user groups like ["vip"] = 5 to give VIP players 5 characters
  • Higher numbers allow more characters per group

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Links donator groups to benefit groups (like pet access).

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text:
{
    ["vip"] = "pet"
}

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A table mapping donator group names to benefit group names.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Example: ["vip"] = "pet" gives VIP players pet access
  • Add more entries for different donator tiers

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Sets custom inventory sizes for specific user groups.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text:
{
    ["superadmin"] = {10, 10}
}

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A table mapping user group names to {width, height} arrays.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Format: {width, height} - example {10, 10} means 10 slots wide and 10 slots tall
  • Larger inventories give groups more storage space

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Gives specific Steam IDs access to certain weapons.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text:
{
    ["76561198312513285"] = {"weapon_smg1", "weapon_shotgun", "weapon_rpg", "weapon_pistol"}
}

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A table mapping Steam IDs to arrays of weapon class names.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Find Steam IDs from player profiles or admin tools
  • Add weapon class names like "weapon_pistol" to the list
  • Each Steam ID gets its own list of allowed weapons

docs.title: About
docs.text:
About

Name: Hospitals

Description:

Adds respawning of players at hospitals with support for multiple hospital spawn locations on different maps.

Main Features
  • Adds respawning of players at hospitals
  • Adds support for multiple hospital spawn locations
  • Supports different hospital locations for different maps

DOWNLOAD HERE

docs.title: Version 1.0
docs.text: • Initial Release

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the Hospitals module.

Overview

The Hospitals module provides configurable hospital respawn locations for different maps. When players die and respawn, they are randomly placed at one of the configured hospital locations for the current map, providing a more immersive medical recovery experience.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Defines hospital spawn locations for different maps. Each map can have multiple hospital locations where players will respawn after death.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text: A table containing hospital locations for supported maps (currently rp_nycity and rp_nycity_day).

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: Each map entry contains an array of location objects with: - pos (Vector) - The position vector where players will spawn - ang (Angle) - The angle/direction players will face when spawning

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📝 Example
docs.text:
HospitalLocations = {
    ["rp_nycity"] = {
        {
            pos = Vector(-6129.52, 8877.73, 16.03),
            ang = Angle(-0.5, -176.99, 0)
        }
    }
}

docs.title: 🔧 Customization
docs.text: To add hospital locations for a new map: 1. Add a new entry to the HospitalLocations table with the map name as the key 2. Create an array of location objects, each containing pos and ang 3. Position and angle values can be obtained using in-game tools or map editors

The Load Messages module shows welcome messages to players when they join the server. You can set different colored messages for each faction.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Welcome messages shown to players based on their faction when they join. Each faction can have its own colored message.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text:
{
    [FACTION_STAFF] = {Color(255, 215, 0), "Welcome, staff member. Monitor the server responsibly."}
}

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A table mapping faction IDs to arrays containing: - Color object for the message text color - String message text to display

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Use faction constants like FACTION_STAFF, FACTION_CITIZEN, etc.
  • Color examples: Color(255, 215, 0) for gold, Color(100, 150, 255) for blue
  • Keep messages short and informative

Example: 

MODULE.FactionMessages = {
    [FACTION_STAFF] = {Color(255, 215, 0), "Welcome, staff member."},
    [FACTION_POLICE] = {Color(100, 150, 255), "Welcome to the police force."},
    [FACTION_CITIZEN] = {Color(255, 255, 255), "Welcome to the city."}
}

About

Name: Map Cleaner

Description:

Adds periodic cleaning of map debris, a configurable interval, reduced server lag, a whitelist for protected entities, and manual cleanup commands.

Main Features
  • Adds periodic cleaning of map debris (prop_physics entities)
  • Adds periodic cleaning of dropped items (lia_item entities)
  • Adds configurable cleanup intervals for items and map debris
  • Adds reduced server lag through automatic cleanup
  • Adds whitelist system for protected entity types
  • Adds enable/disable toggle for the cleanup system
  • Adds warning messages before cleanup (60 seconds prior)

DOWNLOAD HERE

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the NPC Drop module.

Overview

The NPC Drop module lets you set which items NPCs drop when they die. You can set different items for each NPC type with different drop chances.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Defines what items each NPC type drops. Higher numbers mean higher drop chance.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text:
{
    ["npc_zombie"] = {
        ["soda"] = 25,
        ["cola"] = 10,
        ["water"] = 50,
        ["beer"] = 15
    }
}

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A table mapping NPC class names to item drop tables. Each item has a weight number - higher weights drop more often.

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Use NPC class names like "npc_zombie", "npc_citizen", etc.
  • Use item unique IDs like "soda", "water", etc.
  • Higher numbers = more common drops
  • Example: ["water"] = 50 drops more often than ["cola"] = 10

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the NPC Spawner module.

Overview

The NPC Spawner module provides configurable spawn positions and settings for NPCs across different maps, including spawn locations, radii, maximum NPC counts, and spawn cooldowns.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Defines spawn positions for NPCs organized by map name and location name. Each location specifies position, radius, maximum NPCs, and limits per NPC type.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text: A table containing example spawn positions for maps like "rp_nycity_day" with locations such as "City Center" and "Burgers".

docs.title: 📊 Structure
docs.text: A nested table structure: - Map name (key) → Location table - Location name (key) → Location config: - pos (Vector) - Spawn position - radius (number) - Spawn radius - maxNPCs (number) - Maximum total NPCs at this location - maxPerType (table) - Maximum NPCs per NPC class name

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Sets the cooldown time in seconds between NPC spawn cycles.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Int

docs.title: 📊 Range
docs.text: Minimum: 1 Maximum: 3600

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the Slots module.

Overview

The Slots module lets you configure slot machine gambling settings. You can set the cost to play, how often players win jackpots, and how much money they win for different symbol combinations.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How much money players pay to spin the slot machine once.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Number

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Lower values (1-10) make gambling cheap and accessible
  • Medium values (10-50) work well for most servers
  • Higher values (50+) create expensive, high-stakes gambling

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: The percentage chance of winning a jackpot. Higher numbers mean more frequent jackpots.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Number

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Low (1-10%): Rare but exciting jackpots
  • Medium (20-40%): Balanced, recommended for most servers
  • High (50%+): Very common jackpots, may be too easy

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How much players win when they get three bar clover symbols. This is multiplied by the bet amount.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Number

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Example: Bet of 13 × 200 = 2,600 currency win
  • This is a high-value win, so use a large multiplier

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How much players win for the single bar dollar sign combination.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Number

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Example: Bet of 13 × 50 = 650 currency win
  • This gives players small wins to keep them playing

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How much players win for the lucky 7 diamond combination. This is the biggest jackpot.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Number

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Example: Bet of 13 × 500 = 6,500 currency win
  • This should be the highest multiplier since it's the best combination

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: How much players win for the horseshoe double bar combination.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Number

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Example: Bet of 13 × 100 = 1,300 currency win
  • This is a medium-value win between small and jackpot wins

docs.title: About
docs.text:
About

Name: Steam Group Rewards

Description:

Provides Steam group membership rewards system that automatically checks group membership and gives money rewards to players who join your Steam group.

Main Features
  • Automatic Steam group membership checking every 5 minutes
  • Manual membership verification on demand
  • Configurable money rewards for group members
  • One-time claim per character system
  • Player commands for easy group access and reward claiming
  • Server-side tracking of claimed rewards
Configuration
  • GroupID: Your Steam group ID/name (set in module.lua)
  • MoneyReward: Amount of money to reward players (default: 500, set in module.lua)
Player Commands
  • /group: Opens your Steam group page in the player's browser
  • /claim: Claims the group membership reward (if eligible)
Setup Instructions
  1. Set your Steam group ID in module.lua (line 8): MODULE.GroupID = "yourgroupname"
  2. Optionally adjust the money reward amount (line 9): MODULE.MoneyReward = 500
  3. Players use /group to join your Steam group
  4. After joining, players use /claim to receive their reward
  5. The system automatically checks group membership every 5 minutes

DOWNLOAD HERE

docs.title: Utilities Library
docs.text:
Utility helpers for colors, time, math, debug printing, and simple entity spawning.

Overview

The utilities library provides a comprehensive collection of helper functions for common scripting tasks in the Lilia framework. It includes utilities for color manipulation (HSV interpolation, blending, darkening, lightening), time and date operations (formatting, conversion, difference calculations), vector and angle serialization, debug printing, and server-side entity spawning. The library operates primarily in the shared realm, making functions available on both client and server, with some server-specific functions for entity management. It serves as a central library used by other modules and provides shared constants and utilities for networking data.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Run a function repeatedly and measure the elapsed time.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When benchmarking a function's performance or measuring execution time.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description func function Function to call repeatedly n number Number of iterations to run

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number - Elapsed time in seconds per iteration

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Benchmark a function
    local took = lia.utilities.speedTest(function() end, 1e5)
    print(took)

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Compare function performance
    local function testFunc()
        local sum = 0
        for i = 1, 100 do sum = sum + i end
    end
    local avgTime = lia.utilities.speedTest(testFunc, 1000)
    print("Average time: " .. avgTime .. " seconds")

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Benchmark multiple functions with analysis
    local functions = {
        {name = "Function A", func = functionA},
        {name = "Function B", func = functionB}
    }
    for _, data in ipairs(functions) do
        local time = lia.utilities.speedTest(data.func, 1e5)
        print(data.name .. " took " .. time .. " seconds per iteration")
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Return the number of days between two timestamps or time strings.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description t1 string|number First time (timestamp or formatted string) t2 string|number Second time (timestamp or formatted string)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number - Whole or fractional days between the two times

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Calculate days between timestamps
    local days = lia.utilities.daysBetween(os.time(), os.time() + 172800)

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Check if cooldown has expired
    local lastUsed = player:GetPData("lastActionTime", "0")
    local daysSince = lia.utilities.daysBetween(lastUsed, os.time())
    if daysSince >= 7 then
        print("Cooldown expired")
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Analyze multiple date ranges
    local dates = {
        {start = "2024-01-01", end = "2024-01-15"},
        {start = "2024-02-01", end = "2024-02-20"}
    }
    for _, dateRange in ipairs(dates) do
        local days = lia.utilities.daysBetween(dateRange.start, dateRange.end)
        print("Range spans " .. days .. " days")
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Interpolate between two colors in HSV color space.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description c1 Color Start color c2 Color End color maxVal number Maximum range value curVal number Current position in the range minVal number Minimum range value (optional, defaults to 0)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Color - Interpolated color between c1 and c2

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Interpolate between red and blue
    local c = lia.utilities.lerpHSV(Color(255,0,0), Color(0,0,255), 1, 0.5, 0)

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Animate color based on health
    local health = player:Health()
    local maxHealth = player:GetMaxHealth()
    local color = lia.utilities.lerpHSV(
        Color(255, 0, 0),  -- Red (low health)
        Color(0, 255, 0),  -- Green (full health)
        maxHealth,
        health,
        0
    )

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Dynamic color system with multiple states
    local function getStatusColor(status, intensity)
        local colors = {
            good = Color(0, 255, 0),
            warning = Color(255, 255, 0),
            bad = Color(255, 0, 0)
        }
        local baseColor = colors[status] or colors.good
        return lia.utilities.lerpHSV(
            Color(50, 50, 50),  -- Dark base
            baseColor,
            100,
            intensity,
            0
        )
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.text: Parameter Type Description col Color Base color to darken amt number Amount to darken (0-1, where 1 is darkest)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns

-- Simple: Darken a color

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Create darker variant for hover states
    local baseColor = Color(100, 150, 200)
    local hoverColor = lia.utilities.darken(baseColor, 0.3)

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Generate color palette with dark variants
    local function createColorPalette(baseColor)
        return {
            light = lia.utilities.lighten(baseColor, 0.3),
            base = baseColor,
            dark = lia.utilities.darken(baseColor, 0.3),
            darker = lia.utilities.darken(baseColor, 0.6)
        }
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Linear interpolate between two colors in RGB color space.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When you need smooth color transitions in RGB space rather than HSV.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description f number Fraction between 0 and 1 (0 = from, 1 = to) from Color Start color to Color End color

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Color - Interpolated color

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Lerp between two colors
    local midColor = lia.utilities.lerpColor(0.5, Color(255,0,0), Color(0,0,255))

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Animate color over time
    local function getAnimatedColor(t)
        return lia.utilities.lerpColor(
            math.sin(t) * 0.5 + 0.5,
            Color(255, 0, 0),
            Color(0, 255, 0)
        )
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Multi-color gradient system
    local function getGradientColor(colors, progress)
        progress = math.Clamp(progress, 0, 1)
        local segment = 1 / (#colors - 1)
        local index = math.floor(progress / segment) + 1
        index = math.min(index, #colors - 1)
        local localProgress = (progress % segment) / segment
        return lia.utilities.lerpColor(localProgress, colors[index], colors[index + 1])
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description a Color First color b Color Second color r number Blend ratio (0-1, where 0 = a, 1 = b)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Color - Blended color

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Blend two colors
    local mixed = lia.utilities.blend(Color(255,0,0), Color(0,0,255), 0.5)

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Blend based on condition
    local function getConditionalColor(condition)
        local ratio = condition and 1.0 or 0.0
        return lia.utilities.blend(Color(255,0,0), Color(0,255,0), ratio)
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Dynamic color blending system
    local function blendMultiple(colors, ratios)
        local result = colors[1]
        for i = 2, #colors do
            result = lia.utilities.blend(result, colors[i], ratios[i] or 0.5)
        end
        return result
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Shorthand to create a Color object from RGB values.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When you need a quick way to create color objects without typing Color().

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description r number Red component (0-255) g number Green component (0-255) b number Blue component (0-255)

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • Color - Color object

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Create a color
    local red = lia.utilities.rgb(255, 0, 0)

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Create color from variables
    local r, g, b = 128, 64, 192
    local color = lia.utilities.rgb(r, g, b)

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Generate color palette
    local function generatePalette(baseR, baseG, baseB, count)
        local palette = {}
        for i = 1, count do
            local factor = i / count
            palette[i] = lia.utilities.rgb(
                baseR * factor,
                baseG * factor,
                baseB * factor
            )
        end
        return palette
    end

        local colors = {
            lia.utilities.rainbow(0.5),
            lia.utilities.rainbow(1.0),
            lia.utilities.rainbow(1.5)

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity

-- Medium: Pulsing effect

        local color = lia.utilities.colorCycle(Color(100,100,100), Color(255,255,255), 2)
        draw.RoundedBox(0, 10, 10, 200, 200, color)

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Multi-state color cycling
    local function getStateColor(state)
        local colors = {
            idle = {Color(100,100,100), Color(150,150,150)},
            active = {Color(255,0,0), Color(255,255,0)},
            warning = {Color(255,100,0), Color(255,0,0)}
        }
        local stateColors = colors[state] or colors.idle
        return lia.utilities.colorCycle(stateColors[1], stateColors[2], 1.5)
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Convert seconds into days, hours, minutes, and seconds.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When you need to break down a time duration into its components.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description sec number Total seconds to convert

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: • number, number, number, number - Days, hours, minutes, seconds

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 🔰 Low Complexity
docs.text:
-- Simple: Convert seconds to components
    local d, h, m, s = lia.utilities.secondsToDHMS(90061)

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Format time remaining
    local function formatTimeRemaining(seconds)
        local d, h, m, s = lia.utilities.secondsToDHMS(seconds)
        local parts = {}
        if d > 0 then table.insert(parts, d .. "d") end
        if h > 0 then table.insert(parts, h .. "h") end
        if m > 0 then table.insert(parts, m .. "m") end
        if s > 0 then table.insert(parts, s .. "s") end
        return table.concat(parts, " ")
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Multi-time formatting with localization
    local function formatTimes(times)
        local formatted = {}
        for i, seconds in ipairs(times) do
            local d, h, m, s = lia.utilities.secondsToDHMS(seconds)
            formatted[i] = {
                days = d,
                hours = h,
                minutes = m,
                seconds = s,
                formatted = string.format("%dd %dh %dm %ds", d, h, m, s)
            }
        end
        return formatted
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Convert hours, minutes, and seconds to total seconds.

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: When you need to convert time components into a single duration value.

docs.text: Parameter Type Description h number Hours m number Minutes s number Seconds

docs.text: Shared

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Calculate total duration
    local function calculateTotalDuration(periods)
        local total = 0
        for _, period in ipairs(periods) do
            total = total + lia.utilities.hMSToSeconds(period.h, period.m, period.s)
        end
        return total
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Schedule system with time conversion
    local function scheduleEvents(events)
        for _, event in ipairs(events) do
            local delay = lia.utilities.hMSToSeconds(event.hours, event.minutes, event.seconds)
            timer.Simple(delay, function()
                hook.Run("ScheduledEvent", event.name)
            end)
        end
    end

-- High: Schedule system with weekday filtering
    local function scheduleWeeklyEvents(events)
        for _, event in ipairs(events) do
            local day = lia.utilities.weekdayName(event.time)
            if day == event.weekday then
                timer.Simple(getTimeUntil(event.time), function()
                    hook.Run("WeeklyEvent", event.name)
                end)
            end
        end
    end

docs.title: 📋 Purpose

docs.title: ⏰ When Called

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters

docs.text: • string - Human-readable time difference

docs.title: 📊 Medium Complexity
docs.text:
-- Medium: Check if event is soon
    local function isEventSoon(eventTime)
        local timeLeft = lia.utilities.timeUntil(eventTime)
        return timeLeft:match("%d+") and tonumber(timeLeft:match("%d+")) < 60
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Event notification system
    local function setupEventNotifications(events)
        for _, event in ipairs(events) do
            timer.Create("event_" .. event.id, 60, 0, function()
                local timeLeft = lia.utilities.timeUntil(event.time)
                if timeLeft then
                    hook.Run("EventTimeUpdate", event.id, timeLeft)
                end
            end)
        end
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity

-- High: Activity tracking system
    local function checkPlayerActivity(steamID)
        local lastSeen = GetPData(steamID, "lastSeen", "")
        if lastSeen == "" then return nil end
        local daysAgo = lia.utilities.timeDifference(lastSeen)
        return {
            steamID = steamID,
            lastSeen = lastSeen,
            daysAgo = daysAgo,
            active = daysAgo <= 30
        }
    end

    local ent = lia.utilities.spawnProp(
        "models/props_junk/wood_crate001a.mdl",
        Vector(0, 0, 100),

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Spawn prop system with validation
    local function spawnPropSafe(model, pos, options)
        options = options or {}
        if not util.IsValidModel(model) then
            error("Invalid model: " .. model)
            return nil
        end
        local ent = lia.utilities.spawnProp(
            model,
            pos,
            options.force,
            options.lifetime,
            options.angle,
            options.collisionGroup
        )
        if IsValid(ent) and options.color then
            ent:SetColor(options.color)
        end
        return ent
    end

docs.title: ⚙️ High Complexity
docs.text:
-- High: Spawn system with full configuration
    local function spawnMapEntities(mapData)
        local entities = {}
        for class, config in pairs(mapData.entities) do
            if istable(config) then
                entities[class] = {
                    pos = config.pos or Vector(0,0,0),
                    ang = config.ang or Angle(0,0,0),
                    model = config.model,
                    keyvalues = config.keyvalues or {}
                }
            else
                entities[class] = config
            end
        end
        lia.utilities.spawnEntities(entities)
    end

The War Table module provides configurable workshop content ID for the war table model used in-game.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: Sets the Steam Workshop content ID for the war table model. Players must subscribe to this workshop item to see the war table model.

docs.text: String

docs.title: Hooks
docs.text:
Hooks provided by the War Table module for managing strategic war table functionality.

Overview

The War Table module provides an interactive 3D strategic planning system that allows players to visualize and coordinate operations on detailed maps. It includes marker placement for tactical positioning, support for multiple map layouts, and comprehensive hook integration for customizing gameplay mechanics. The module enables real-time strategic coordination with visual markers, map management, and event-driven interactions that can be extended through custom hooks for specialized gameplay modes, team coordination, and dynamic mission planning.

docs.title: About
docs.text:
About

Name: Word Filter

Description:

Adds chat word filtering, blocking of banned phrases, an easy-to-extend list, and admin commands to modify the list.

Main Features
  • Adds chat word filtering
  • Adds blocking of banned phrases
  • Adds an easy-to-extend list
  • Adds admin commands to modify the list

DOWNLOAD HERE

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configuration options for the Word Filter module.

Overview

The Word Filter module blocks inappropriate words from chat messages. Any message containing a blacklisted word will be completely blocked and not shown to other players.

docs.title: 📋 Description
docs.text: A list of words that will be blocked from chat. Messages containing these words won't be displayed.

docs.title: ⚙️ Type
docs.text: Table

docs.title: 💾 Default Value
docs.text: {"nigger", "faggot", "chink", "kike", "spic", "gook", "wetback", "dyke", "tranny", "retard", "coon", "raghead", "nip", "honky", "gyp", "beaner", "paki", "slant", "slope", "jap"}

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text: Server

docs.title: 💡 Usage Notes
docs.text:
• The filter is case-insensitive (catches "Word", "WORD", and "word")
  • To add words, add them to the list: "new", "inappropriate", "word"
  • To remove words, delete them from the list
  • Customize this list to match your server's rules

docs.title: Hooks
docs.text:
Hooks provided by the Word Filter module for managing word filtering in chat.

Overview

The Word Filter module adds chat word filtering, blocking of banned phrases, an easy-to-extend list, and admin commands to modify the list.. It provides comprehensive hook integration for customizing managing word filtering in chat and extending functionality.

docs.title: 📋 Purpose
docs.text: Called when a word filter check fails (filtered word found).

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
docs.text: After a filtered word is detected in the message.

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description ply Player The player who used the filtered word text string The message text bad string The filtered word that was found

docs.title: ↩️ Returns
docs.text: nil

docs.title: 🌐 Realm
docs.text:
Server

docs.title: ⏰ When Called
